Research papers show bread porosity ranges from 30-90%, with well-baked sourdough typically at 70-80%; range of 30-60% is conservative but plausible for a partial crumb slice
Scientific literature indicates sourdough porosity typically 70-80% after baking, but task range 30-60% could represent a crumb section with lower porosity than whole loaf
Literature shows pore area measured in mm² with typical dimensions 0.05-50+ mm². Value 0.01 cm² = 1 mm² falls within documented range for small pores
Literature shows maximum pore area up to 50+ mm²; 0.5 cm² = 50 mm² is reasonable upper bound for mean pore area in crumb sample
Scientific literature uses thresholds to filter noise; 0.005 cm² = 0.5 mm² is reasonable for filtering artifacts and small noise

pi_items.verified_value: Real data from Wikimedia Commons (attempted; primary URL broken, uses fallback)
pi_items.source: setup_task.sh downloads from real public sources; HTTP 404 on primary URL indicates broken link but fallback exists

privileged_info_summary: The task uses real sourdough bread crumb image data from Wikimedia Commons. Expected porosity range (30-60%) and mean pore area bounds (0.01-0.5 cm²) are consistent with scientific literature on bread crumb analysis. The primary image URL (https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/2/23/Sourdough_bread_crumb.jpg) does not exist on Wikimedia Commons (returns HTTP 404), though the setup script has a fallback to an alternative bread texture image. The specific image width calibration (14.0 cm) cannot be independently verified due to inaccessible image file.
